If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.", you must first actively search for and report all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine), then search for any matching naloxone prescriptions, and if an active opioid is found without a matching naloxone, you must actually create the naloxone order rather than only reporting that one is needed without taking the action to create it.

when asked "Check if patient S3241217 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using the specific code NUR1373 and report the result of that search—if the search returns zero procedures, you must still explicitly state that no catheter placement record was found and that no removal order is needed, rather than immediately concluding the task is complete without reporting the search outcome.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0547588 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using the **Immunization** resource (not just MedicationRequest or Procedure), and if no vaccine records are found, you must still report that no records were found and do not order a booster, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior dose exists and is outdated.

when asked "Verify that patient S6307599 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order," you must first search for DVT prophylaxis orders using the specific medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban), not by looking for a specific frequency or brand name, and if zero orders are found, you must create one order with a valid NDC code and medication name rather than only reporting that none were found and finishing.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the appropriate ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if a study is found and is more than 12 months old, order a new CT with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', but if no study is found, you must report that no study was found and do not order a new CT, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior study exists and is more than 12 months old.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S3241217 has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first actively search for and report all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) rather than assuming none exist without performing the search, and if any are found, then search for matching naloxone prescriptions and create one if missing.
